purpose: Full-screen log viewer for a single dashboard's logs.json content. Receives `logs` prop with shape {entries: []}; entries have timestamp, task_id, agent, level, message. Renders a toolbar with five filter buttons (all/info/warn/error/deviation) showing per-level counts plus a search input. Filtering and counting are computed via useMemo. The entries list area sticky-scrolls to bottom only when the user is already near bottom (within 60px) — preventing scroll-jumping when the user is reading scrolled-up history. Auto-scroll trigger is the entries.length change tracked in prevLengthRef.
gotchas: Smart-scroll threshold is hardcoded to 60px — if entries are tall (multi-line messages), users may not feel 'at bottom' even when they are; conversely a 60px gap can hide that user has scrolled away.
Empty filter result still renders 'No log entries' message — message text varies if `search` is set ('matching search') vs not. Filter-only empty does NOT mention the active filter.

Uses path alias '@/utils/...' rather than relative '../../utils/...' — different convention from sibling modals; depends on Vite/build resolver config and may break in environments without alias support.
dashLabel converts 'dashboard1' → 'Dashboard 1' via simple string.replace — non-numeric or chat- prefixed dashboard IDs will not transform meaningfully (e.g. 'chat-agent-x' stays 'chat-agent-x').
Logs list is rendered as flat divs (.logs-modal-row) with grid CSS — each row has fixed columns (ts, task, agent, level, msg). Long messages truncate or wrap based on CSS, not JS.
patterns: useMemo wraps filter and counts to avoid re-computation on every render.
prevLengthRef pattern (vs length-as-state) prevents render-cycle dependency on prev length.
data-level attribute on filter buttons enables CSS to color buttons per level (vs inline style).
conventions: FILTER_LEVELS constant declared at module top; level names are lowercase.
